article.title: Uber OA 15th June 2025
article.content:
There were 3 coding questions and you have to solve them in 60 minutes.

Q.1 Yousef is faced with the task of defusing N bombs, numbered from 1 to N. Each bomb i has a timer and will detonate in Yi seconds. To neutralize bomb i, Yousef needs Xi seconds. He must finish defusing each bomb before or exactly when its timer runs out. Importantly, he can only work on one bomb at a time. Knowing this in advance, Yousef has asked for your assistance. Your goal is to determine whether it's possible for him to defuse all the bombs in time. If it is, return the minimum total time required to complete the defusals. Otherwise, return -1.

([Xi, Yi], where Xi represents time taken by Yousef to defuse ith bomb and Yi represents time by which ith bomb will explode)

Input: N=5, bombInfo=[[2, 4], [1, 9], [1, 8], [4, 9], [3, 12]]
Output: 11

Constraints: 1<=N<=2*10^5

---

Q.2 You are given an array of n magical fruits. The power of the fruit is given in an array a of length n. You are also given an integer k. Your task is to find the maximum length L such that every subarray of length L has the sum of powers strictly less than k.

Input Format
The first line contains two integers n and k — the number of fruits and the maximum allowed power sum. The second line contains n integers — the array a, where a[i] is the power of the i-th fruit.

Output Format
Print a single integer — the maximum value of L.

Constraints
1 ≤ n ≤ 2 * 10^5
1 ≤ a[i] ≤ 10^9
1 ≤ k ≤ 10^18

Input: a=(3, 1, 2, 4), k=8
Output: L=3

---

Q.3 At the Robotics Innovation Campus "UBERBOT", Unit IG-0R is a vertical wall-climbing robot that can scale surfaces using embedded magnetic contact points (nodes). The robot is deployed on a vertical metal wall designed as a rectangular grid with:

n horizontal levels (rows)
m segments per level (columns)
Each segment is either:
'X' (node the robot can use)
'#' (empty space)

Valid Route Criteria
Start: The first node must be on the bottommost level (row n).
End: The last node must be on the topmost level (row 1).

<!-- ![image.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/2a98cbaf-e20b-435b-be39-563b1ff4b9e1_1749993458.6889677.png) -->
Level-by-Level Progression:
The robot may only move upward or horizontally; it cannot move to a lower level. The robot must access at least one node per level to recharge its navigation sensors.

Node Usage per Level:
The robot can use up to two nodes per level (due to sensor synchronization constraints). These nodes can be used in any order, but each node is visited at most once in a route.

Battery Constraint:
IG-0R's battery capacity allows it to jump only between nodes whose Euclidean distance is less than or equal to w, unit range.

The distance between two nodes at positions (i1, j1) and (i2, j2) is computed as:
sqrt((i1 - i2)^2 + (j1 - j2)^2)


The robot cannot transition between nodes if the computed distance exceeds its battery limit w.
w >= sqrt((i1 - i2)^2 + (j1 - j2)^2)

Example:
N:3 M:4 W:1

Grid
XX#X
#XX#
#X#X

Expected Output: 2
<!-- ![image.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/55699b9d-202a-49a8-8858-a4a4979693e8_1749993531.4288085.png) -->
Constraints:
2<=n<=2000
1<=m<=2000
1<=w<=2000
a integer containing the number of distinct valid routes, computed modulo 998244353

post.content: How did you apply? What role?

i solved the first one fully and got 3/5 test cases working in 2nd and 3/6 working in 3rd.\nIt\'s difficult to get shortlisted I suppose considering it was a mass hiring and with all the people in telegram helping but I did it honestly to the best of my knowledge.
Q1 -> Binary Search\nQ2 -> 2 pointers\nQ3 -> DFS with memo
The 3rd one was interesting problem wherein normal dfs will not work as it will give TLE, memoization along with dfs will optimally work for this.
